items.headline: Two new single-family homes permitted on Chamberlain Drive
items.summary: Permits issued for two 2-story houses at 3550 and 3554 Chamberlain Dr, totaling over $1 million in new residential construction.

items.headline: Fifth Third Bank signage permitted at 250 Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The bank received approval to install three illuminated LED signs on its downtown Palo Alto location.

items.headline: City to study eliminating chemical pesticides on public property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review a policy to stop using chemical pesticides on land owned or leased by Sunnyvale. This affects parks, open spaces, and other municipal properties where residents spend time.

items.headline: Artificial turf policy study underway at Sustainability Commission
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission scheduled a study session on artificial turf policy. The outcome will shape whether and how the city uses synthetic turf on parks and recreational grounds.
